let’s quit documentation¶

Add your content using reStructuredText syntax. See the reStructuredText documentation for details.

class app.my_profile.TargetDateTime(date, time)¶

Bases: object

A class that manages and converts target date and time into a datetime object.

app.my_profile.calculate_stats(target_dt, wage)¶

Calculates the remaining time until the target time and returns the converted stats.

app.my_profile.countdown()¶

Real-time countdown page — parameters:

  • name: date in: query type: string required: true

  • name: time in: query type: string required: true

  • name: wage in: query type: integer required: false default: 9860

responses:
200:

description: Renders the real-time countdown page.

app.my_profile.get_conversion_stats(diff_seconds, wage)¶

Calculates the expected earnings, and the number of chickens and coffees that can be purchased based on the remaining time.

Parameters:

  • diff_seconds – Remaining time (seconds)

  • wage – Hourly wage to apply (KRW)

Returns:

Expected earnings, number of chickens, number of coffees (tuple)

app.my_profile.get_diff_seconds(target_dt, now)¶

Calculates the difference between the target time and current time in seconds.

Parameters:

  • target_dt – Target date and time (datetime object)

  • now – Current date and time (datetime object)

Returns:

Remaining time (in seconds, 0 if in the past)

app.my_profile.get_request_params()¶

Extracts target date, time, and wage from the request’s query parameters.

app.my_profile.index()¶

Main page (Target setting) — responses:

200:

description: Renders a form where users can input the target date and time.

app.my_profile.stats()¶

Witty conversion stats page — parameters:

  • name: date in: query type: string required: true

  • name: time in: query type: string required: true

  • name: wage in: query type: integer required: false

responses:
200:

description: Renders a page showing expected earnings, and the number of chickens and coffees.

let's quit
